top_methods: Structural Equation Modeling (SEM), Theory of Planned Behavior (TPB) extension
top_tasks: Predict pedestrians' violating road-crossing behavior intentions
top_datasets: Survey data from 260 respondents in Dalian, China
narrative: The appendix records 1 papers and 5 matrix rows used to generate the review synthesis.

The most visible methodological families are Structural Equation Modeling (SEM), Theory of Planned Behavior (TPB) extension, while recurring tasks include Predict pedestrians' violating road-crossing behavior intentions.

evidence_table.title: An extension of the theory of planned behavior to predict pedestrians' violating crossing behavior using structural equation modeling
methods: Structural Equation Modeling (SEM), Theory of Planned Behavior (TPB) extension
tasks: Predict pedestrians' violating road-crossing behavior intentions
datasets: Survey data from 260 respondents in Dalian, China
claims: Instrumental attitude was a significant predictor of pedestrian violating crossing behavior intention across all four models tested
Descriptive norm was a stronger predictor than subjective norm, and when descriptive norm was added to the model, subjective norm became non-significant
Conformity tendency was a significant predictor in Model 4, indicating that pedestrians are more likely to cross against traffic lights when they observe others doing so
